  • Agent Thinking: A new execution runtime replacing the legacy orchestration engine. It enables true agentic behavior: multi-step planning, tool calling, human-in-the-loop approval, and secure sandboxed execution — far beyond classic question-and-answer interactions.
  • Joule Studio 2.0: A managed development environment to build your own enterprise agents and skills, grounded in SAP's Knowledge Graph and deployable to SAP's infrastructure. Deep dive into the new Joule Studio and watch the demo.

The EAC programs are your opportunity to use these capabilities before GA, work directly with SAP's product teams, and influence the final feature set.

Q2 Is this 'Joule 2.0'? How does it relate to the Joule I already have?

There is no product called 'Joule 2.0.' It is the same Joule — the core AI assistant technology has not been replaced or versioned. What has changed is how Joule is provisioned and experienced:

  • Provisioning change — SAP has moved from customer-managed Joule (where the customer set up Joule components in their own BTP tenant) to SAP-managed provisioning and Joule and SAP-managed integrations to products (SAP provisions and operates everything). This is a change to how you receive Joule, not a new product.
  • Joule Work — This is a new standalone interface that bundles Joule's existing AI capabilities with new features like Spaces and the front-end for Joule Studio, creating a single point of entry for SAP's key AI functionality. It complements, rather than replaces, classic Joule.

If you hear the term 'Joule 2.0' internally or from customers, it most likely refers to Joule Work — the new unified interface. Correcting this terminology matters because it avoids the impression that existing Joule investments are being deprecated or replaced with a completely separate product.

Q3 What Joule EAC programs are currently available after Sapphire 2026?

SAP launched four separate EAC programs. Each covers a distinct Joule product area:

  • Joule Work & Joule Studio — The new AI-native engagement layer (Conversations + Spaces) and the AI-first development environment for building agents. Registration ends July 15, 2026. (Campaign #3986)
  • Joule with Knowledge Graph — Joule grounded in SAP's Business Knowledge Graph for precise, context-aware answers — plus upcoming agentic reasoning and voice capabilities. Registration ends October 1, 2026. (Campaign #4219)
  • SAP Business AI Agents — A broad portfolio of autonomous AI agents across HCM, Finance, Spend, Supply Chain, and CX. Continuously open registration. (Campaign #3987)
  • Joule Work Desktop App — A standalone desktop app for enterprise knowledge workers with local file access, web search, MCP integrations, and custom skills. Registration ends July 12, 2026. (Campaign #4218)

You may register for one or more programs. Registering is an expression of interest only and does not guarantee participation.

Q4 The Joule Work EAC and the Business AI Agents EAC both mention agents — what's the difference?

This is a common point of confusion. Here is how to distinguish them:

  • Joule Work & Joule Studio EAC (#3986) — Focuses on the experience layer and the development platform. You use Joule Work as your unified workspace (Conversations + Spaces), and with Joule Studio you build custom agents and skills for your own organization. The agents here are ones you create or configure yourself.
  • SAP Business AI Agents EAC (#3987) — Focuses on SAP-built, domain-specific agents that come ready to use out of the box — pre-built assistants for specific business functions like a Payroll Assistant, a Sourcing Assistant, or a Deal Closing Assistant. Joule Assistants are role-based orchestrators that translate user intent into action by coordinating Joule Agents — which are the underlying, task-specific AI workers that autonomously execute complex, multi-step business workflows using tools, data, and reasoning.

An important nuance: the SAP Business AI Agents (Autonomous Assistants) only require Joule Work if they are system-triggered — meaning the agent initiates an action automatically based on a system event, without a user starting the conversation. User-initiated interactions with autonomous assistants do not require Joule Work. If you are evaluating autonomous agents, clarify whether your target use case is system-triggered or conversational, as this determines whether Joule Work is a prerequisite. All agents — whether system-triggered or conversational — require SAP-managed Joule, as it provides the Agent Gateway that powers agent execution. SAP-managed Joule can be set up independently of any EAC program via SAP for Me, and can either replace an existing classic Joule deployment or run side-by-side with one.

In short: #3986 is about building and experiencing the platform; #3987 is about consuming SAP's catalog of ready-made agents. Many customers will want both eventually.

Q5 Can we join multiple EAC programs at the same time?

Yes — the four EAC programs are independent and stackable. You can register for and participate in more than one simultaneously, provided you meet the individual prerequisites for each. The most common combinations are:

  • Joule Work & Joule Studio (#3986) + Business AI Agents (#3987) — for customers who want the new UX layer and SAP's pre-built domain agents.
  • Joule Work & Joule Studio (#3986) + Knowledge Graph (#4219) — for customers who want the new UX layer with deep grounding in their SAP data.
  • Desktop App (#4218) as an add-on — it has the lightest prerequisites and can run independently of the other three programs.

Note that each program has its own onboarding, project team requirements, and feedback commitments. Make sure you have the internal capacity to engage meaningfully with each program you join.

 

2 — Registration & Process

Q6 Where and how do I sign up?

All four programs are hosted on the SAP Influence portal at influence.sap.com. Log in with your SAP Universal ID, search for the campaign by name or number, and click 'Register.' Registration deadlines:

  • Joule Work & Joule Studio — Register by July 15, 2026 (Campaign #3986)
  • Joule with Knowledge Graph — Register by October 1, 2026 (Campaign #4219)
  • SAP Business AI Agents — Continuously open (Campaign #3987)
  • Joule Work Desktop App — Register by July 12, 2026 (Campaign #4218)

If you experience issues with the portal, SAP recommends using the Chrome browser. For support, contact your SAP Account Executive or the EAC Program Lead.

Q7 I submitted my registration — what happens next, and how long until I get access?

Submitting the registration form is an expression of interest only. The process after registration:

  • SAP reviews your application against program criteria and available capacity. This typically takes a few weeks.
  • If accepted, SAP contacts you (via your Account Executive or the EAC Program Lead) to confirm participation and discuss onboarding.
  • Onboarding includes verifying prerequisites (e.g., SAP-managed Joule migration if needed), signing the EAC agreement, and scheduling a kickoff with your dedicated EAC coach.
  • If not selected in the first round, SAP may contact you as capacity opens.

For time-sensitive programs (Joule Work & Joule Studio closes July 15; Desktop App closes July 12), register as soon as possible to maximize your chances of being considered in the first intake.

Q8 When will Joule Work reach General Availability (GA)? Can we plan around a specific date?

SAP has not published a final, committed GA date for Joule Work or the other EAC programs. GA timing is intentionally contingent on the outcome of the EAC programs — SAP uses the co-innovation phase to validate readiness before committing to GA.

As directional guidance: EAC programs are expected to run for 3–6 months before GA. Based on the Joule Work & Joule Studio EAC targeting a June 2026 start and running 180 days, a Joule Work GA in the Q4 2026 timeframe is the current directional target. For other programs with longer EAC windows, GA is expected in 2027.

For planning purposes, do not commit to hard internal deadlines based on these dates. Use the EAC period itself to build readiness, develop use cases in Joule Studio, and train your team — so you are ready to scale at GA regardless of the exact date.

Q9 How long do the EAC programs last?

Program durations differ per campaign. The Joule Work & Joule Studio EAC runs for 180 days from the program start (targeting a July 2026 launch, ending approximately mid-November 2026). The Knowledge Graph and Business AI Agents programs have longer or rolling windows. Exact end dates per customer depend on when participation is confirmed.

The EAC roadmap progressively unlocks additional Joule Work capabilities:

  • August 2026: Discover feed, Jobs supervision, System-generated Spaces (autonomous assistants), Joule Action Bar / Omnipresence
  • November 2026: Shared Spaces (multi-user + agent collaboration), Scheduled Jobs, Voice (web), Compositional Analytics

 

3 — Eligibility & Prerequisites

Q10 Is EAC participation free? What will Joule Work cost after General Availability?

EAC participation itself is free — there is no direct charge for the early access period. However, you must hold the relevant Joule entitlements and have signed the terms & conditions (available via SAP for Me). Depending on the track, a Joule Studio license and/or Joule Work Spaces activation may be required.

Pricing for Joule Work beyond the EAC has not yet been finalized and will be communicated separately ahead of GA. EAC participation does not commit you to any specific commercial terms for GA. Raise commercial questions with your SAP Account Executive early if they are a gating factor.

Q11 We are on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or on-premise — can we join the EAC, and do we need to migrate first?

S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (PCE) customers can participate in EAC programs — and importantly, you do not necessarily need to fully migrate away from customer-managed Joule before joining. SAP's recommended approach for existing customer-managed Joule customers is a hybrid brownfield setup: you register for the EAC and new Joule components (such as Joule Work) are set up in an SAP-managed way, while your existing customer-managed components remain connected. You do not have to do a full cutover before the EAC starts.

Program-by-program eligibility for Private Cloud customers:

  • Joule Work & Joule Studio (#3986) — SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition may become eligible with future product enhancements. At EAC launch, S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is the primary supported system. Register and SAP will assess your eligibility.
  • Joule with Knowledge Graph (#4219) — S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is explicitly listed as an eligible Joule setup. This is the most accessible EAC for Private Cloud customers.
  • SAP Business AI Agents (#3987) — Private Cloud and Public Cloud are both in scope. Note that specific agents and assistants require a certain S/4HANA Private Cloud version. A compatibility list of agents by S/4HANA Private Cloud version is available for PCE Finance and Procurement (May 2026). Contact your SAP account team or visit our agent roadmap for agent-specific version requirements.
  • Joule Work Desktop App (#4218) — No S/4HANA dependency at all, and equally no S/4HANA connectivity — you cannot read from or write to any SAP backend system. The app works with local files and external services via MCP tools only. The most broadly accessible program.

On-premise customers: On-premise S/4HANA customers can connect their system to Joule — and by extension to Joule Work — provided they meet specific criteria: a RISE contract and a commitment to the 40% maintenance fee. If these conditions are met, on-premise is a viable path into the EAC programs. The Knowledge Graph EAC (#4219) and Desktop App EAC (#4218) are the most relevant entry points.

Q12 We are a SuccessFactors-only customer — which EAC programs can we join?

SuccessFactors-only customers have access to specific EAC programs:

  • Joule Work & Joule Studio (#3986) — Not available at EAC launch. Joule Work Spaces currently requires SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. SuccessFactors integration into Spaces is planned but not in the initial EAC scope.
  • Joule with Knowledge Graph (#4219) — Open to SuccessFactors customers. Joule with SAP SuccessFactors is an explicit prerequisite option. Recommended EAC for SF-only customers.
  • SAP Business AI Agents (#3987) — Open. The Autonomous HCM domain is designed primarily for SuccessFactors customers — Core HR, Payroll, Recruiting, Performance & Goals, Learning, and more.
  • Joule Work Desktop App (#4218) — Open. No SuccessFactors or S/4HANA dependency, and equally no connectivity — you cannot read from or write to any SAP backend system..

Summary for SF-only customers: register for Knowledge Graph (#4219) and/or Business AI Agents (#3987) as your primary paths.

Q13 Is 'SAP-managed Joule' a prerequisite — and what exactly does it mean?

SAP-managed Joule is a prerequisite for the Joule Work & Joule Studio EAC and for the Business AI Agents EAC. It is SAP's new SaaS delivery model where SAP provisions, hosts, and operates the entire Joule infrastructure on your behalf — replacing the older customer-managed model where customers maintained Joule components in their own BTP tenant.

If you are currently on customer-managed Joule, an upgrade is required before full Joule Work participation. However, SAP's recommendation is a hybrid brownfield approach: new Joule Work components are set up SAP-managed, while your existing customer-managed setup remains connected during the transition. You do not need to complete a full migration before registering for the EAC — raise your current setup with SAP during the acceptance process.

Q14 Can our system integrator or implementation partner participate in the EAC with us?

Yes — the Joule Work & Joule Studio EAC (Campaign #3986) is explicitly designed for customers and partners. System integrators and implementation partners can participate alongside the customer, which is the recommended approach if a partner is involved in your rollout.

  • Your partner can be involved in onboarding, configuration, and Joule Studio agent development.
  • The customer organization remains the primary accountable party — the dedicated project team and point of contact must be from the customer side.
  • Partners should coordinate with the customer and the SAP Account Executive during the acceptance process.

For the other three programs, partner involvement is not explicitly mentioned but is reasonable in an implementation capacity. Confirm with your SAP EAC Program Lead during onboarding.

 

4 — Joule Work & Joule Studio EAC (Campaign #3986)

Q15 What is the Joule Work & Joule Studio EAC and what can I experience?

This is the flagship EAC for the new Joule Work experience. It covers two products:

  • Joule Work — A full screen entry point framework connecting users to capabilities across SAP through intent, context, and conversational AI. The EAC includes Conversations (conversational AI with read and write access) and Spaces (intent-based, generative workspaces aggregating data across your SAP landscape).
  • Joule Studio — A fully managed, AI-first development environment for building enterprise AI agents, apps, and workflows. Supports intent-based development, open tooling (VS Code, Cursor, Vercel, n8n, LangGraph, LlamaIndex), and deploys to a secure SAP-managed runtime with enterprise governance, persistent agent memory, and access to the SAP Knowledge Graph.

You can participate in both together (Joule Studio license + Joule Work Spaces activation required) or in Joule Work only (Spaces activation required). Combined participation is recommended for teams who want to build and consume custom agents.

Q16 What are the prerequisites and known limitations for this EAC?

Prerequisites:

  • Signed terms & conditions for Joule and the $0 SKU for Joule Work / Joule Studio
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as primary LoB system
  • SAP-managed Joule (hybrid brownfield setup possible — see Q13)
  • Joule Work Spaces activation (required for all participants)
  • Joule Studio License (required for the combined track only)
  • A defined use case and dedicated internal project team with assigned point of contact

Known limitations at EAC launch:

  • Joule Work Spaces supports SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition only
  • Only one S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system per tenant (no multi-system)
  • Custom agent integrations limited to SAP-managed and SAP-native services — no third-party system integrations
  • Document grounding not supported
  • Spaces are initially read-only (write actions via Spaces in a subsequent update)
  • Datacenter Regions: USA and EU only

 

5 — Joule with Knowledge Graph EAC (Campaign #4219)

Q17 What is the Joule with Knowledge Graph EAC and who should apply?

This EAC gives customers access to Joule grounded in the SAP Business Knowledge Graph — enabling specific, context-aware answers based on your actual system data rather than generic AI responses, covering the full range of SAP entities. The program also gives early access to upcoming innovations including agentic reasoning and voice capabilities.

Prerequisites — an existing Joule setup with one of: SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, SAP SuccessFactors, or SAP Ariba. Your Joule instance must be in the EU10 or US10 data center (more coming soon). Registration closes October 1, 2026.

This program is the recommended entry point for Private Cloud and SuccessFactors-only customers.

 

6 — SAP Business AI Agents EAC (Campaign #3987)

Q18 What is the SAP Business AI Agents EAC and which agents are available?

This is a broad, continuously running EAC covering SAP's portfolio of autonomous AI agents across five business domains:

  • Autonomous HCM — Career & Talent Development, Compensation, Core HR, HR Knowledge, HR Service, Learning, Onboarding, Payroll, People Intelligence, Performance & Goals, Recruiting, Skills, Time, Workforce Upskilling.
  • Autonomous Finance — Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, Billing, Cash & Treasury, Financial Consolidation, Expense Management, Financial Closing, Financial Planning, Governance, Tax & Compliance.
  • Autonomous Spend — Buying, Category Management, Procurement Contracts, Invoicing, Sourcing, Supplier Management, Travel.
  • Autonomous Supply Chain — Asset & Service, Business Network, Logistics, Manufacturing, Planning, Product Design.
  • Autonomous CX — Campaign, Case Management, Deal Closing, Order Management, Sales, Service Management, and more.

Prerequisite: an existing Joule setup hosted in the EU10 data center (additional data centers coming). Note that specific agents require a certain S/4HANA Private Cloud version — consult your SAP account team for version-specific compatibility. Detailed prerequisites per individual assistant will be published by SAP in the near future. Registration is continuously open.

Important: the Autonomous Assistants only require Joule Work if they are system-triggered (agent initiates action based on a system event). User-initiated conversations with these agents do not require Joule Work.

 

7 — Joule Work Desktop App EAC (Campaign #4218)

Q19 What is the Joule Work Desktop App — and how is it different from the browser-based Joule Work?

Joule Work Desktop is a standalone native desktop application for enterprise knowledge workers. It is a distinct product from the browser-based Joule Work and is explicitly not integrated with the Joule Work web/cloud version.

Key capabilities: 

  • Conversational AI interface (on-device)
  • Local file & workspace access within a secure sandbox
  • Artifact generation — documents, summaries, structured outputs
  • Terminal command execution and integrated web search
  • MCP Server integration — connects to SAP and non-SAP systems via Model Context Protocol
  • Custom / corporate skills for repetitive tasks

Prerequisites:

  • SAP for Me access including an S-User ID
  • Requires download and installation of a binary executable on the user's machine — MacOS and Windows supported
  • A defined use case and dedicated project team
  • No existing Joule setup required — broadest accessibility of all four programs

Key limitations:

  • No integration with Joule Work (web/cloud)
  • No out-of-the-box SAP backend system integrations
  • Not available under EU-Access restrictions — must not be used for EU-only work purposes

Registration closes July 12, 2026.

 

8 — Trust, Data & Security

Q20 Where does our data go during the EAC — and is it used to train SAP's AI models?

Data governance is a top concern for enterprise customers. SAP's commitments in this area:

  • No training on customer data — SAP does not use customer business data to train or improve its AI models without explicit customer consent. This is a core SAP Business AI principle and applies to all EAC programs.
  • Data residency — For cloud-based EAC programs, data is processed and stored in your designated SAP data center (EU10 or US10). The data center region of SAP-managed Joule can be chosen during initial set-up
  • Joule Work Desktop — local data — The Desktop App accesses local files within a secure sandbox. Files are not automatically uploaded to SAP cloud systems. Review the specific EAC terms for what telemetry, if any, is collected.
  • Agent actions & HITL — All agent actions with business impact in Joule Work require explicit human approval (Human-in-the-Loop) before execution. No agent writes to your SAP systems without your confirmation.
  • Verifiability — Every action taken by Joule Work can be verified in the backend via traditional SAP Fiori apps, and AI recommendations include reasoning so users can validate before acting. All agent actions are written to the SAP Audit Log, providing a full traceability trail for compliance and governance purposes.

For contractual data protection terms, refer to the SAP Data Processing Agreement and EAC-specific terms signed during onboarding.

 

9 — Support & Next Steps

Q21 What support does SAP provide during the EAC — and what happens when the program ends?

All EAC participants receive dedicated co-innovation support:

  • Monitoring of critical issues by an EAC Project Coach and/or the development organization
  • Direct feedback channels — surveys, feedback sessions, and community forums — feeding directly into the Joule product teams
  • EAC-specific release notes and product update communications
  • Onboarding support to configure prerequisites and set up your first Spaces, skills, or agent scenarios

After the EAC: participants transition automatically to the GA versions. Your configurations, Spaces, and skills carry over. Pricing for Joule Work beyond the EAC has not yet been finalized and will be communicated separately. EAC participation does not commit you to any specific commercial terms for GA.

Continue Your Joule Learning Journey

As the Joule portfolio continues to evolve, investing in learning today will help your organization accelerate adoption and maximize value when these innovations become generally available. Here are several recommended resources to deepen your understanding and help your teams prepare for adoption:

  • Understand SAP's AI Strategy: Review the latest Sapphire 2026 announcements related to Joule, autonomous agents, and business AI innovation.
  • Learn about Joule: Introducing Joule Learning Journey focused on Joule capabilities and business user adoption.
  • Stay engaged through our AI enablement formats:
    • Beyond Hype, AI live session format, which is all about getting productive with SAP Business AI
    • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills Monthly Blog Series spotlights the latest AI learning assets and upcoming learning opportunities.

The latest Joule innovations are more than new features, they reflect SAP’s vision for an AI-powered, agent-driven enterprise.

Whether you're exploring Joule Work or Joule Studio, the Early Adopter Care programs offer a unique opportunity to gain early hands-on experience and collaborate directly with SAP product teams.

If you're considering participation, start with a clear business use case, review the prerequisites, and engage your SAP account team early. The most successful EAC participants are those who actively co-innovate and provide feedback that helps shape the future of these solutions.

All EAC programs are expressions of interest only and do not create binding commitments by SAP. Functionality, dates, and prerequisites are subject to change. For the latest information, visit influence.sap.com or contact your SAP Account Executive. This document reflects the program scope as of June 2026.

entry.title: Talk to Your Localized Reports: How Joule Runs A/P, A/R, and Stock Reports in Native Language
entry.content:
Running a country-specific aging report used to mean remembering the transaction code, the exact parameter names, and the local compliance rules that shape every field. Now you can just ask.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces AI-assisted localized business data management — a new capability that lets Joule, SAP's generative AI digital assistant, run classic localized reports through natural-language prompts while respecting every country- and region-specific requirement.

Scope Note

This blog post covers SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the new AI Case Manage Business Data Using Localized Classic Applications. Activation requires the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG and the corresponding business catalog per report. Three localized reports are currently in scope: the A/P Aging Report, the A/R Aging Report, and the Stock Overview Report. The reports were originally built for specific countries, but any customer can adopt them wherever they see a fit — simply copy the delivered business catalog into your own business role and assign it to the target users. Other editions (on-premise, Cloud Private Edition) and other localized reports are not covered here.

Main Insight

Localized reports are where compliance meets complexity. Every country encodes its own rules for aging buckets, currency handling, cleared-item treatment, and inventory posting — and each rule shows up as another parameter on a classic report screen.

The result: teams spend more time configuring the report than reading it. New users take weeks to get productive. And every parameter mis-entry is a potential compliance gap.

AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management flips that. Joule sits on top of the same classic reports your finance and supply-chain teams already use — but it understands the country-specific context. You describe what you need in plain English. Joule fills the parameters, respects the localization rules, and runs the report.

You keep the compliance-certified backend. You lose the friction.

Three Reports, One Conversational Interface

Three classic localized reports ship with the initial release. Each requires its own business catalog assigned to the user.

1. Accounts Payable (A/P) Aging Report

  • Report ID: RFIDCN_AP_AGING
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by company code, supplier, and key date · select item types · configure due-date buckets and currency · analyze amounts in transaction or reporting currency
  • Example prompt: "Run the A/P Aging Report for specific suppliers or supplier ranges within a company."

2. Accounts Receivable (A/R) Aging Report

  • Report ID: RFIDCN_AR_AGING
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAR_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by company code, customer, and key date · include or exclude cleared and special G/L items · define due-date buckets and reporting currency
  • Example prompt: "Run the A/R Aging Report for customers S13100251 to S13100253 in company 1310."

3. Stock Overview Report

  • Report ID: J_3RMOBVEDH
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_MOBVED_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by plant and product number · select a posting date or date range · filter by storage location and batch · review inventory status in context
  • Example prompt: "Run the Stock Overview Report for product tg-d010_ua in plant 6510 with posting date today."

Same reports. Same compliance guarantees. New way to reach them.

Not restricted to the original country. These reports ship as SAP-delivered business catalogs, but you don't have to run them only in the country they were originally designed for. Copy the delivered catalog into a custom business role and assign the role to any user or business unit that needs the same reporting logic. For step-by-step guidance, see Copying and Editing Business Roles — SAP Help Portal.

Activation in 6 Steps

The capability is off by default. Enable it with the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG:

  1. Open your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition configuration environment with an administrator user that has the required authorization.
  2. Search for and open the configuration activity Activate Features.
  3. In the feature list, search for the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG.
  4. Review the feature details to confirm you are activating the AI-assisted localized business data management capability.
  5. Choose the activation option and confirm when prompted.
  6. Wait until the system confirms successful activation, then assign the required business catalog(s) to your users.

Once activated, users can invoke Joule from any Joule-enabled entry point in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and prompt the localized reports directly.

Practical Takeaway

Start with one report and one country. Pick the localized report your team runs most often — A/P Aging for a China entity is a good candidate — and follow this path:

  • Verify your system runs SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Joule enabled
  • Activate feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG in a test tenant
  • Assign the matching business catalog (e.g. SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC) to a pilot user
  • Ask Joule to run the report using one of the example prompts above
  • Compare Joule's parameter selections to what your team would have entered manually
  • Roll out to the full team once you confirm the compliance rules and outputs match

You'll know it's working when your finance team stops asking "which parameter goes where?" and starts asking "can I get the same report for last month?" — because now they can.

Summary

Localized reports carry the weight of country-specific compliance, and that complexity usually lives in the report screen. AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management moves the complexity into Joule — users describe what they need in natural language, and the classic report runs with the correct localized parameters. Three reports ship first (A/P Aging, A/R Aging, Stock Overview), with more expected as the capability expands. Because the reports are delivered as SAP business catalogs, you can copy them into your own business roles and reuse them wherever the reporting logic fits your process — not only in the country they were originally built for.

Key Takeaways

  • Compliance stays in the backend. Joule doesn't reinterpret local rules — it drives the existing compliance-certified classic reports, so audit and regulatory guarantees remain intact.
  • Activation is a single feature toggle. GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG in the Activate Features configuration activity turns the capability on. No custom development required.
  • Business catalog assignment is per report. Each of the three initial reports has its own catalog (SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC, _IDCNAR_PC, _MOBVED_PC) — assign only what each user needs.
  • Productivity gain is immediate. New users skip the parameter-memorization phase. Experienced users skip the parameter-entry step. Both groups reach the same compliant result faster.
  • Scope is intentionally narrow at launch. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition only, three reports only. Plan pilots around what's shipped today, and monitor SAP Help Portal for the expanding scope.
  • Reuse beyond the original country. Copy the delivered business catalogs into custom business roles and assign them wherever the reporting logic fits — the reports aren't locked to the country they were originally designed for.

More Information

  • AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management — SAP Help Portal
  • Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition — SAP Help Portal
  • Activating Business AI and Assigning Users — SAP Help Portal
  • Copying and Editing Business Roles — SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Joule in SAP PaPM UM Cloud: Light or Intensive - Choose Right Before You Switch It On
entry.content:
AI gets all its applause for what it can do.

Answer questions, explain an untraceable number, guide a user through a process, and remove an hour of manual work.

But none of that happens in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Universal Model Cloud the moment Joule shows up on the screen. It happens when one quieter thing is true first: the connection is set up correctly.

Here’s the part most teams skip past - there isn’t one way to connect Joule. There are two. And picking the wrong one for how you actually plan to use AI is the whole difference between “it just works” and “why is it so slow, why did that fail, why can’t I get in?”

So before you switch anything on, answer one honest question: Are we using AI lightly, or are we about to lean on it every single day?

Let’s walk both paths.

Path 1: Light Usage - It’s Already In the Box

Here’s the good news that saves a lot of effort: SAP Profitability and Performance Management Universal Model Cloud already ships with built-in AI, ready to use through a preinstalled Joule client. No dedicated AI Core, no model to deploy, no destination to wire. It’s already there - you don’t build the connection, you just start asking.

There’s one rule you have to respect, and it’s there by design. A Fair Use Policy applies per day, per tenant. Stay inside light usage and everything runs smoothly. Push past it - too many requests, too fast - and automatic throttling steps in to protect the shared capacity.

Think of it as a shared lane on a highway. Perfectly quick when traffic is normal, but it isn’t your private road. Flood it, and the system deliberately slows you to keep things fair for everyone else on the tenant. That single fact is what makes light usage perfect for occasional AI - and the wrong fit for heavy, all-day work.

To be clear, light usage isn't a preview - it's the full experience. Here are some assistants you'll find across the product:

  1. Report and Simulation Assistant: Adjust a what-if scenario inside a report (set the What-If percentage for Account Training to 90 and watch the plan-and-forecast update).
  2. Process and Activities Assistant: Drive processes by conversation-activate a process for an environment, confirm it's live, and see how many activities it carries.
  3. Analysis Assistant (in the Show screen): Ask plain-language questions about the data ("what's the total for this GL account this fiscal year?") and get precise, context-aware answers.
  4. Model Assistant: Build your model by describing it - create environments, functions, fields, and more, without clicking through every screen.
  5. Knowledge Assistant: Ask how things work and get answers grounded in product knowledge, so guidance is right where you're working.
  6. Connection Assistant: Set up and check your connections conversationally, so data sources and integrations come together with less manual effort.

These are a few examples: Joule appears in more places, and its capabilities keep expanding.

If you use light, the entire “setup” comes down to three things:

  1. Confirm your subscription is active and that the Joule client is available.
  2. Ensure that you are assigned the required roles.
  3. Enter your request in Joule and proceed with your task.

If that's you, you can stop reading here - you're done, and you didn't build a thing.

Path 2: Intensive Usage - Bring Your Own Engine

But what if AI isn’t an occasional helper? What if it’s becoming part of daily operations - many users, many questions, all day? Then the shared lane stops being enough, and the guidance is blunt about it: for intensive usage, set up and connect to a dedicated SAP AI Core to ensure high speed. This is where you stop borrowing capacity and start running your own engine - more headroom, better performance, more control.

Heads-up on regions. Create and use your AI Core instance in the same region as your current PaPM instance - and only where AI Core is actually supported. The supported regions today are ap10, ap11, ap20, ap30, br10, eu10, eu11, eu20, eu30, in30 jp10, us10, us21, and us30. Confirm your PaPM region is on this list before you start.

Once it’s in place, the rest is one clean flow through four stages - and the order is the whole game:

 

① Build the engine - SAP AI Core

The first stage stands up the engine that actually runs the models, and it ends with the single most important artifact in this whole process: a service key. Make sure you’re on the AI Core Extended plan here - that’s the one that unlocks generative AI.

Here’s how to set it up:

  1. Create a subaccount in SAP BTP (You can use a different subaccount from the one used for SAP Profitability and Performance Management.)
  2. Enable the Cloud Foundry environment in the subaccount.
  3. Create a space within the Cloud Foundry environment.
  4. Add the required service plan for SAP AI Core.
  5. Create a service instance of SAP AI Core.
  6. Create and download the service key for the service instance.

Treat that key like the crown jewels. The URL, client ID, and client secret inside it are exactly what you’ll paste into the destination two stages from now. Everything downstream reads from this one file - save it somewhere safe and don’t lose it.

See detailed setup steps : Build the engine - SAP AI Core 

② Deploy the model - SAP AI Launchpad

A model you’re entitled to isn’t automatically up and running. Stage two closes that gap. Start by choosing the AI Launchpad Standard plan, then set it up just like you did with SAP AI Core - create a subaccount, set up Cloud Foundry, and add the service plan. From there, subscribe to the service, assign yourself the necessary access, and launch it.

Now the real work, and it's a short sequence. First, add a connection to SAP AI Core using the service key from Stage ①. Then create a configuration: give it a name, choose the foundation-models scenario, and pick the model you want to serve.

From that configuration, create a deployment - the live endpoint that will actually answer requests.

If this is your very first Launchpad, you'll also need to create the supported embedding model, text-embedding-ada-002 - the integration depends on it.

And then you wait for one word. Open the deployment and watch its status until it reads “Running.” Need more than one model? Just repeat the configure-and-deploy loop for each.

This is the gate for everything that follows. If the deployment isn’t “Running,” the destination you build next has nothing on the other end - and Joule will look connected while every question quietly vanishes.

See detailed setup steps : Deploy the model - SAP AI Launchpad 

③ Build the bridge - the BTP destination

With a running model, stage three connects PaPM to it through an HTTP destination in the SAP BTP Cockpit. You’ll head into your PaPM subaccount, open Connectivity → Destinations, and create a new one from scratch.

This is the one place where the exact values matter, because a typo here is invisible until Joule silently fails - so slow down. And here’s the detail you can’t get wrong: the name is not free form. For Joule in the Universal Model, the destination must use the predefined name PAPM_UM_AI_UNIVERSAL. Get a single character wrong and the integration won’t find it.

With the name set, the rest is transcription. Set the type to HTTP, proxy type to Internet, and authentication to OAuth2ClientCredentials. The URL is your service key’s API URL with /v2/inference on the end; the Client ID and Client Secret come straight out of that same service key; and the Token Service URL is the key’s URL with /oauth/token appended, using a Dedicated token service type. Leave the two mTLS / basic-credentials checkboxes unticked.

Then comes the part that actually decides how Joule behaves - the Additional properties. This is where you choose the models, so it’s worth getting right. There are four:

configuration Parameters

 
Parameter

Requirement

Description

ai-resource-group

Required

Your AI Launchpad workspace’s resource group. Often just default.

ai_modelRequiredThe primary model Joule reasons with. Recommended: anthropic--claude-4.6-sonnet, claude-4.6-opus, claude-4.5-sonnet, or claude-4.5-opus.
websearch_modelOptionalSet it to sonar or sonar-pro to let Joule pull real-time web search into its answers.
ai_support_modelOptionalA lighter secondary model (anthropic--claude-4.5-haiku or gemini-2.5-flash) that handles the simpler tasks alongside the primary one.
 

Fill those in, then save.

Every one of those service-key values is sitting in the file you downloaded in Stage ①. This stage is really just transcribing that key carefully into the right boxes - under the one name the integration expects - and choosing which models do the thinking.

See detailed setup steps : Build the bridge - the BTP destination 

④ It’s already live - tenant-wide

Here’s the quietly satisfying part: there’s no final “now point PaPM at it” step to fumble. Because the system finds the connection by its fixed name, the moment you save PAPM_UM_AI_UNIVERSAL, it applies across every environment in the tenant. No per-environment wiring, no name to copy into a settings screen, nothing left to forget.

That’s the whole handshake. The engine is running, the model is deployed and “Running,” and the destination is in place under the name Joule’s Universal Agent goes looking for. Joule goes live - only now it’s drawing on your own dedicated AI Core. Same assistant your users see; far more room to breathe behind it.

A Word on Capacity - Size It Before You Sign
That €10k minimum buys Capacity Units, and how far they stretch depends entirely on which model you run and how hard you run it.

The lesson hiding in that trade-off is simple - capacity is a function of the model. A chatty model with long answers burns far more than a lean one. So don’t guess. SAP’s Generative AI hub Cost Calculator lets you plug in your model, your token sizes, and your monthly volume and tells you what you’ll actually consume. Run that before you finalize the BTPEA, not after the invoice surprises you.

How to Choose - In One Breath

If your AI use is occasional, stay light: it’s already built in, you respect the daily Fair Use Policy, you accept that heavy bursts get throttled, and you start today without building anything.

If AI is becoming part of daily business, go intensive: confirm your region is supported, get the BTPEA in place, then walk the four stages in order - engine, model, bridge, live - naming the destination exactly PAPM_UM_AI_UNIVERSAL and choosing your models in the destination’s properties, and size your Capacity Units against the model you actually intend to use.

The real mistake isn’t choosing one over the other. It’s not choosing - drifting into heavy use on the light model and wondering why it throttles, or paying for a dedicated engine you don’t yet need.

Final Thought

The best AI experiences feel effortless to the person using them. They almost never get that way by accident.

Behind a smooth Joule moment in Profitability and Performance Management Universal Model Cloud, there’s a decision someone made on purpose: light or intensive, fair-use lane or dedicated engine, the right model sized to the real workload - and, when it’s intensive, four stages walked in the right order.

Get the quiet groundwork right, and Joule stops being a button in the corner of the screen. It becomes the assistant you actually reach for.

For the complete, official step-by-step instructions, see the SAP Help Portal 

To understand Joule capability, see the Introducing the Joule in Universal Model for SAP PaPM: When Your Model Finally Talks Back..

entry.title: SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions of AI for Your Implementation
entry.content:
This is the first blog post in a series introducing the topic of AI in SAP Activate. Follow this blog as we will update it with links to other posts as they are released.

Last update: July 15th 2026, with links to additional related blog posts at the end of the article.

In this blog post, I want to share how SAP Activate is evolving with SAP Business AI and what is already available to you today to leverage in your implementation projects. As SAP expands the availability of AI capabilities and more assistants and agents become available for our customers, SAP Activate is keeping pace to ensure you have the guidance you need to adopt, extend, and apply these capabilities effectively during your journey to the Autonomous Suite.

When we think about Business AI in the context of SAP Activate, we organize it into three interconnected dimensions. Each dimension addresses a different aspect of how AI applies in the implementation lifecycle. In the rest of this post, I will walk you through each of these dimensions, explain what is available today, and highlight how you can start benefiting from these capabilities right away.

Three Dimensions of Business AI in SAP Activate

Before I get into the details, let me introduce the framework. The three dimensions are:

  1. Adopting Business AI capabilities and SAP Joule by SAP customers during their implementation and beyond.
  2. Extending SAP solutions with AI capabilities and agents built on the SAP Business AI Platform.
  3. Leveraging AI capabilities for implementation activities.

These dimensions are interconnected but address different audiences and project stages. Let me expand on each one.

1. Adopting SAP Business AI and SAP Joule

The first dimension is about guiding you, the customer, to activate, set up, and adopt SAP Joule and SAP Business AI capabilities as part of your implementation. In other words, this is about integrating AI into how your organization runs its business processes in the SAP Autonomous Suite.

Think of it this way: when your project team starts scoping the implementation and later runs fit-to-standard workshops, they should be considering not only the standard business processes but also the AI capabilities that come with them. Which processes benefit from AI-powered automation or recommendations? Where does SAP Joule provide value to your end users in their day-to-day work? What agents and assistants would enrich the business processes in our organization? These are the questions SAP Activate helps you answer.

Already in 2025, SAP Activate was enhanced with guidance to help customers activate, set up, and adopt SAP Joule and available SAP Business AI capabilities (see the Business AI catalog on SAP Discovery Center). The guidance in SAP Activate includes self-enablement tasks, guidance for running fit-to-standard workshops with AI considerations, and procedures for setting up the necessary integrations. As an example, the information for the setup of SAP Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is available here (the tasks are tagged with Joule tag).

How do you find this content? You can select the SAP Business AI and SAP Joule tags in the road map viewer in SAP for Me to identify the relevant tasks for your implementation scenario. The flow is straightforward: identify the available SAP Business AI capabilities and SAP Joule features, assess and validate them during fit-to-standard, activate and set up during Realize, and enable your business users to adopt the new capability.

Example of a task for the initial setup of Joule in SAP Cloud ERP Private

We plan to continue expanding this coverage throughout 2026, with increased focus on the new AI capabilities and agents as they become available to customers, and to incorporate steps to consider and embed these capabilities into the scope of your implementation.

Dimension 2: Extending with SAP Business AI Platform Services

The second dimension is for organizations that need to go beyond the pre-built AI capabilities. It supports customers in leveraging the SAP Business AI Platform to build custom AI solutions and agents that extend standard SAP application capabilities in line with the clean core strategy.

This is an important distinction: the first dimension is about adopting what SAP delivers out of the box. The second dimension is about building your own AI-powered extensions on the platform when your business requires something unique. The key principle here is that these extensions follow the clean core approach, ensuring your solution remains upgradeable and maintainable.

SAP Activate for SAP Business Technology Platform already covers various AI-related topics, including guidance for application development on SAP BTP and implementation guidance for AI capabilities. The use of platform services to extend solution guidance is also embedded in the implementation roadmaps for our ERP solutions in the Extensibility work stream, for both private and public cloud solutions. You can access it here.

The flow in your project follows the extensibility work stream: identify and confirm extensibility requirements during fit-to-standard workshops (or earlier if the requirements are already known), leverage SAP Business AI Platform services to extend the standard solution and test during the Realize phase, and finally enable your business users to adopt the new capability.

We plan to continue evolving this implementation guidance as new platform capabilities and services become available, including the new agents and assistants announced at SAPPHIRE 2026.

Dimension 3: Leveraging AI Capabilities for Implementation

The third dimension is where I expect many of you reading this post will find the most immediate value. This is about applying AI capabilities throughout your implementation project to accelerate requirements gathering, design, development, testing, and knowledge transfer across the project lifecycle.

This dimension covers a broad range of AI capabilities like Joule on road map viewer, SAP Joule for Consultants, and embedded AI capabilities in the integrated toolchain, including SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and WalkMe.

AI-Assisted Tasks

Earlier in June, SAP Activate released the first wave of AI-assisted implementation tasks. This initial release included 9 updated SAP Activate tasks, 13 new tested prompts, and updated accelerators, focused on SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

These AI-assisted tasks are designed to support your project team in leveraging AI capabilities during implementation. Rather than focusing on information retrieval, we focused on high-impact tasks and deliverables where AI can make a real difference. SAP Activate delivers tested prompts and example outputs, and in some cases, updated accelerators.

The initial focus is on Joule for Consultants, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP Test Automation Tool through all project stages, covering seven front-runner topics:

  1. Self-enablement on Joule for Consultants – helping your team get started with the capability.
  2. Project execution and artifacts preparation – accelerating the creation of project management deliverables.
  3. Preparation for fit-to-standard workshops – using AI to help consultants prepare effectively for these critical sessions.
  4. Generating requirements from fit-to-standard workshop artifacts – processing workshop results into requirements and user stories in SAP Cloud ALM with assistance from Joule for Consultants.
  5. Generating design documents for extensions – using AI to draft extension design documentation.
  6. Preparing for data loads  – leveraging AI to help with data migration strategy and approach.
  7. Testing – preparing testing strategy and approach, as well as automating preparation of test scripts, determination of test scope, and management of testing.

An AI-assisted task includes two key elements: an AI-assisted procedure section in the task description that explains how to use the AI capability, and a link to the prompt library. The prompt library provides access to the prompt text, example output, and additional details for prompting the AI tool. We built a centralized prompt library to maintain the integrity of prompts linked to SAP Activate and to ensure all users can access them, even if they do not yet have direct access to the AI capability where a dedicated prompt library may exist.

You can find these tasks by filtering for the AI-Assisted Procedure tag in the SAP Activate roadmap viewer or SAP Cloud ALM when accessing the Task Management app. The SAP Activate team will continue to expand the coverage with AI-assisted tasks in the future. Let us know your feedback on the current tasks and your suggestions for areas to enhance.

Joule in SAP Activate Road Map Viewer on SAP for Me

I also want to highlight a capability that has been available since June 2025 and continues to bring great value to our users. SAP Joule is embedded directly in the SAP Activate road map viewer on SAP for Me. This is a conversational AI assistant that enables you to surface the guidance you need through natural-language questions, eliminating the need to manually navigate the roadmap structures.

You can ask Joule your SAP Activate-related questions, carry out a conversation, and copy the answers to share with your partners or colleagues. For example, you might ask about the sequence of activities for data migration, or about which accelerators are available for a specific topic. Joule will provide a clear response based on the SAP Activate content.

To try it, navigate to the SAP Activate road map viewer and click on the Joule icon in the top right corner. You can read more about this capability in the blog post from Chandan: Say Hi to Joule in SAP Activate road map viewer.

Outlook

Looking ahead, we will continue enriching SAP Activate with AI guidance for implementation experts – project managers, consultants, developers, and architects – on how to use available AI capabilities during projects.

In the mid-to-long term, we will evolve SAP Activate as new agents and assistants become available in SAP Cloud ALM, aligned with the agentic AI transformation platform vision shared at SAPPHIRE 2026. We anticipate significant evolution as new agents are built and integrated into the offerings through 2026.

I encourage you to explore the AI-assisted tasks in the road map viewer, try Joule on SAP for Me, and share your experience. Please use the comments to this blog post to continue the conversation or reach out to us at sapactivatemethodology@sap.com with your feedback and suggestions. We are excited to hear how you are leveraging these capabilities in your projects.

Other blogs in this series

To deep-dive into AI-assisted tasks and capabilities, access the blogs linked below (we will add the links as the blogs get published).

  • Introducing the SAP Activate Prompt Library: Curated AI Prompts for Your Implementation Project by Chandan Gurav
  • AI-Assisted Extensibility Planning & Design in SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud by Kiran Bose
  • Work Smarter with AI-Assisted Tasks in the SAP Activate Project Management Workstream by Christina Galbreath
  • SAP Activate, AI and Data Management by Daniel Ciecko
  • Transform Your Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation with AI-Assisted Procedures by Jair Azevedo

Useful Resources

  • SAP Activate road map viewer
  • Introducing Joule
  • Setting Up Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscapes
  • Introducing SAP Joule for Consultants
  • Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape

entry.title: Joule for Developers
entry.content:
Joule's ABAP developer capabilities support full-stack development in ABAP Cloud. It provides real-time explanations of development objects and is fully integrated in the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, offering accurate information about open objects or marked code. Joule predicts and generates subsequent lines of code based on ABAP Doc, comments, or code being typed, taking the ABAP language version into account to suggest fitting code. It can also generate end-to-end ABAP cloud development scenarios including all required objects.

SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities is designed to support you with your day-to-day tasks when working with ABAP code, to increase your efficiency and to lower total cost of development.  In the SAP ecosystem specifically across SAP BTP (ABAP Environment) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Joule matters because it understands the proprietary context of ABAP Cloud, Core Data Services (CDS), and SAP Fiori.

You can explore and understand in detail extensively regarding the capabilities of Joule by referring to the blog - Joule Capabilities for Developers ABAP - SAP Community but this blog details on how you can get onboard and capitalise this AI developer journey.

How to get started?

This AI feature is available as a free of charge Promo until October 2026 after which it will be a Premium AI feature. 

Required Parent SKUs (The Foundation)

To activate the Joule for Developers add-on, your global account must have the underlying infrastructure provisioned. 
Customer SKU  : 8019164
Partner SKU      : 8019670

 

 

This promotional license for Joule for Developers  was sunset as of June 1,2026 . The SAP Note 3571857 for the SAP BTP ABAP environment and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and it  described the activation and enablement (commercial and technical) of the promotional license for SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities. No new orders can be placed. Existing contracts will be fulfilled until end of September 2026 at the maximum.

A new commercial model for SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities was introduced, based on AI units, see SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/3740750. 


Sharing our latest blog on SAP Joule for Developers , ABAP AI capabilities, now available through a new AI Unit-based commercial license.
The blog covers:
• Licensing and commercial aspects
• Backend configuration and enablement steps and SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities in ABAP Development Tools for  Eclipse and Visual Studio Code
🔗SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities inc... - SAP Community

The capabilities of Joule for Developers are much more extensive than just basic code generation. Here is a comprehensively updated, tabularized "Feature Deep Dive" based strictly on the extracted official capabilities:

Feature

What it does

How it works technically / Examples

Joule Chat

Acts as an AI pair programmer that you can interact with using natural language for development tasks.

Uses IDE Context Awareness to read currently opened resources. It answers general ABAP questions, explains specific code snippets, and dynamically generates formatted code examples you can directly insert.

Predictive Code Completion

Automatically generates inline "ghost text" to predict and complete code as you type.

Listens to cursor position and editor context. It supports ABAP interfaces, classes, and programs. It is powered by a fine-tuned model (Star Coder 2) trained specifically on SAP internal ABAP source code.

Explain Capability

Provides detailed, semantic explanations of selected development objects or lines of source code.

Fully integrated into ADT. You can select an ABAP class, a CDS artifact, or lines of code, and Joule provides an English explanation. It also supports explaining Custom Code Migration (ATC) findings directly in-app.

Documentation Chat

Allows you to chat directly with public documentation hosted on the SAP Help Portal.

Accesses standard guides (ABAP Keyword Documentation, RAP, ABAP Data Models, ABAP Cloud) and provides referenced answers considering your current project context.

ABAP Unit Test Generation

Automatically creates and refactors ABAP Unit tests to check functional correctness.

Generates tests for public/protected/private methods. It also performs Dependency Analysis to find test-unfriendly dependencies, automatically adds Test Doubles, and helps split or refactor large test classes.

CDS Unit Test Generation

Generates automated test classes and context-specific test data for ABAP CDS entities.

Provides basic test coverage by reading the semantics of a CDS entity and using AI to generate test method names, test data, and updatable test doubles.

OData UI Service from Scratch

Generates a complete RAP BO (Business Object) and UI service from an empty package.

You prompt Joule with requirements (e.g., "Create a Travel BO with two entities"). It automatically generates the database tables, CDS views, behavior definitions, metadata extensions, and service bindings.

RAP Business Logic Prediction

Generates complex implementation logic for RAP determinations and validations.

Triggered via a quick fix inside an empty method. You provide a natural language description (e.g., what to validate, error messages, edge cases), and Joule generates the exact ABAP code for the behavior pool.

Consume Capability

Auto-generates OData requests and the corresponding ABAP code to consume remote services.

You prompt Joule (e.g., "I want to read the field ID from all campaigns"). It outputs the HTTP GET request and the specific ABAP logic required to use the OData Client Proxy.

Embedded Analytics Star Schema Generator

Uses AI to derive and create an analytical star schema based on existing CDS view entities.

Automatically generates the Cube definition (center of the star schema), dimension fields (e.g., Product, Region), and measure fields required for complex ABAP analytics.

GenAI-Driven Extensibility Assistant

Assists developers in executing various tasks related to field extensibility.

Helps find business contexts, usages, value help views, and enables business scenarios when creating custom fields for extensibility.

Custom Code Migration AI Assistant

Accelerates the migration of legacy custom code to modern, clean-core standards.

Integrates with the "Analyze Custom Code / Custom Code Migration" Fiori app to provide deep AI explanations of ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit) simplification findings and legacy processing logic.


Stay tuned as many more features are soon rolling out.  

References:

  • SAP Discovery Center AI Feature - SAP BTP, ABAP environment, SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
  • SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities | SAP Help Portal










References:

  • SAP Discovery Center AI Feature - SAP BTP, ABAP environment, SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
  • SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Introducing the SAP Activate Prompt Library: Curated AI Prompts for Your Implementation Project
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series that introduces AI in SAP Activate. For more information, visit the main blog "SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions of AI for Your Implementation."

One of the most common questions we hear from implementation consultants who are getting started with AI is: "Where do I begin? What exactly do I type?"

It is a fair question. The value of a tool like SAP Joule is immense — but only if you know how to prompt it effectively. Writing a good prompt for a complex, context-rich task like preparing fit-to-standard workshops, drafting a project charter, or building a data migration approach takes time and expertise. And without a starting point, many teams either default to generic, underpowered prompts or simply do not use the capability at all.

Today, we are solving that problem. We are excited to introduce the SAP Activate Prompt Library — a centralized, curated collection of implementation-ready prompts, available directly on SAP for Me.

 

What Is the SAP Activate Prompt Library?

The SAP Activate Prompt Library  is a portal hosted within the SAP Activate road map viewer  on SAP for Me. It provides a structured, searchable collection of prompts crafted specifically for SAP implementation project tasks, aligned to the SAP Activate methodology.

Every prompt in the library has been developed, tested, and validated by the Experts and Architects. They are not generic AI prompts — they are precision-engineered instructions that put Joule into the exact role and context your project team needs, aligned to specific phases, roadmaps, and deliverables.

The prompts are designed for use with SAP Joule, the AI assistant purpose-built for SAP implementation professionals. Joule allows your consultants and project team members to interact with an AI that understands the SAP implementation landscape — and the prompts in this library are designed to get the very best out of it.

How to Access the Prompt Library

The Prompt Library is available to all users through the SAP Activate Road Map Viewer on SAP for Me. Navigate to: https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer/promptlib 

Fig: Homepage

No additional setup is required to browse the library. To use the prompts in SAP Joule for Consultants, you will need access to that capability in your SAP environment.

A Walkthrough of the Prompt Library

The Main View: Browse and Filter

The library is fully filterable across four dimensions, making it easy to surface exactly what you need:

  • Roadmap: SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP; Private – New Implementation; Private – Selective Data Transition; Private – System Conversion; S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Joule Type: Filter by the specific Joule capability being used
  • Phase: Prepare, Explore, Realize, and others
  • Task: Filter by the type of implementation task

You can also search by keyword and save your most-used prompts to a Favorites list for quick access.

Fig: Filters

 

Opening a Prompt: Three Layers of Value

Clicking on any prompt card opens a detail panel with three key sections.

The Prompt

When you open the Prompt Library, you are presented with a card-based view of all available prompts. Each card shows you:

  • The prompt title — a clear, task-oriented name (e.g., "Create Project Charter and Scope Statement", "Prioritize Delta Requirements")
  • The SAP Activate phase — Prepare, Explore, Realize, etc.
  • The target roadmap — which SAP Activate implementation scenario the prompt applies to
  • The target Joule tool — currently SAP Joule for Consultants, we are expanding rapidly
  • A preview of the prompt context — so you can quickly assess relevance without opening the card

Fig: Prompt Selection

 This structure ensures the AI response is grounded, specific, and actionable — not generic. You can view the prompt in the detail panel or click Expand for a full-screen view. A Copy button lets you instantly copy the prompt text to paste into Joule for Consultants.

Fig: Expand Prompts

Expert Template (where applicable)

Some prompts in the library are designed to work with Experts in SAP Joule for Consultants. Experts are custom AI personas that are configured in Joule for Consultants with a specific role, expertise, and behavior set — essentially giving Joule a specialized identity for a particular type of work.

For these prompts, the library includes the full Expert Template: the system-level instructions you paste into Joule for Consultants to create or configure the Expert. For example, the "Create Project Charter and Scope Statement" prompt is designed for a Project Manager Expert — an Expert persona with 15+ years of SAP implementation experience, trained to think in terms of project risks, dependencies, and structured governance deliverables.

The Expert Template is available directly in the prompt detail panel with a single Copy Instructions button — making it fast to set up the Expert and start working.

Fig: Expand Experts

Sample Output

One of the most valuable features of the Prompt Library is the included Sample Output for each prompt. This is a real example of what a well-formed response from Joule looks like when the prompt is used correctly — complete with the expected document structure, depth of content, and formatting.

Sample outputs serve multiple purposes: they help you calibrate your expectations, validate that your Joule session is producing quality results, and can serve as reference examples for stakeholders who are new to AI-assisted delivery.

Fig: Expand Sample Output

What's Next

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is a living resource. The SAP Activate team will continue expanding the library with new prompts across additional phases, roadmaps, and task areas — aligned with the ongoing evolution of SAP Joule for Consultants and the broader SAP Business AI capabilities.

We are also expanding the coverage of Expert Templates, as new expert personas are developed and validated for implementation scenarios beyond project management — including solution architects, functional consultants, data architects, and more.

The library is one part of SAP Activate's broader commitment to Dimension 3 of Business AI: leveraging AI capabilities throughout the implementation lifecycle to accelerate delivery, improve quality, and empower your project team.

Get Started Today

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is available now at https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer/promptlib.

Browse the prompts, explore the Expert Templates, and try them in your next project task. We would love to hear how you are using the library and what additional prompts would be most valuable to you. Please share your feedback in the comments below or reach out to us at sapactivatemethodology@sap.com.

entry.title: Mastering HR Data in the AI Era: Why Peter Aiken’s Framework matters for successful AI adoption
entry.content:
The AI revolution in HR isn’t coming. It’s here. But it only works for organizations that have done the foundational work. Those that didn’t are discovering that you can’t bolt AI onto broken data foundations.

In the AI age, HR data is no longer just administrative—it’s a strategic asset that powers predictive insights, personalized employee experiences, and competitive advantage. In my other blog: Why HR Data Management Strategy is important in your HR Transformation, I described Peter Aiken’s framework for Data Management and how it relates perfectly with the journey SAP SuccessFactors customers go thru during their HR transformation. Peter Aiken, President of DAMA International and a leading voice in data strategy, provides a proven framework that shifts organizations from reactive data fixes to proactive governance.

Peter Aiken’s Framework: The Five Pillars

SAP's Autonomous HCM reveals why Aiken’s data management capabilities aren’t theoretical luxuries but operational necessities. Joule’s ability to deliver accurate natural language insights, automate workflows, and provide contextual recommendations depends entirely on the data foundation you’ve built.

Joule’s Core Data Requirements Mapped to Aiken’s Framework

1. Technical Infrastructure (Aiken’s Foundation Layer)

Joule demands enterprise-grade technical readiness:

  • SAP SuccessFactors license in supported data centers with proper entitlements
  • Identity Authentication Service (IAS) integration for secure user authentication using OAuth2 or SAML
  • SAP BTP destinations configured properly (LPS_SFSF_rt, HTML5.Dynamic Destination)
  • OData API access to Employee Central portlets (Personal Info, Job Info, Address Info, CompoundEmployee)

Without Aiken’s technical infrastructure capability: These integrations fail, Joule can’t access employee data, and your AI investment produces zero value.

2. Logical Infrastructure (Master Data Relationships)

Joule leverages the CompoundEmployee API to understand how employee records connect across modules:

  • How job information links to compensation data
  • How performance reviews relate to goal progress
  • How organizational hierarchies map to reporting structures

Without Aiken’s logical infrastructure: Joule receives fragmented data and can’t provide holistic insights. For example, it might recommend a promotion without seeing the complete performance history because the relationships weren’t properly mapped.

3. Data Quality (The Make-or-Break Factor)

Joule performs optimally only with:

  • Complete Employee Central data via CompoundEmployee API
  • Standardized portlet data (Job Info, Personal Info, Compensation)
  • Clean metadata definitions via OData/SFAPI Data Dictionaries
  • Audit trails and version control on all HR documents

Incomplete records, inconsistent naming conventions, or missing relationships produce unreliable AI outputs.

Without Aiken’s data quality capability: Joule delivers "garbage in, garbage out." Incomplete Job Info portlets lead to flawed promotion recommendations; missing performance lineage creates unreliable AI agents.

4. Metadata Management (AI Transparency)

Joule relies on documented metadata for accuracy:

  • Document Grounding: Indexed HR documents (contracts, policies, handbooks) for retrieval-augmented generation, preventing AI hallucinations
  • Data Dictionaries: OData and SFAPI dictionaries that define what each data element means
  • Real-time Context: Current role, location, permissions, and performance history to personalize responses

Without Aiken’s metadata management: Joule can’t distinguish between outdated policies and current ones, potentially giving employees incorrect information about benefits or compliance requirements.

5. Governance Guidelines (Trust and Compliance)

Joule inherits your governance framework:

  • Role-based permissions determine what data each employee can query
  • Audit trails track every AI interaction for compliance
  • Data stewardship ensures accountability when AI makes recommendations

Without Aiken’s governance capability: Joule might expose confidential compensation data, violate GDPR, or make recommendations based on unauthorized data access.

SAP SuccessFactors offers the technical platform, but success depends on the data management discipline you bring. When implemented together:

  • Employee records become predictive assets rather than administrative burdens
  • Joule transforms from expensive novelty to trusted advisor
  • HR leaders shift from reactive firefighting to proactive strategy
  • AI delivers measurable ROI instead of joining the 80% failure statistic

Joule isn’t just another HR tool—it’s the convergence point where decades of data management best practices meet cutting-edge AI capabilities. Peter Aiken’s framework provides the exact blueprint for this convergence.

Start with Aiken’s iterative approach:

  1. identify your highest-value AI use case
  2. map the required data dependencies
  3. implement the five capabilities systematically
  4. expand from success.

When you ask Joule about promotion eligibility, workforce planning scenarios, or policy interpretations, you’re not just testing an AI—you’re testing whether your organization has mastered data as a strategic asset. The organizations that invested in Aiken’s capabilities years ago are now reaping AI dividends.

Want to know more about how you can get your data ready to adopt AI? Get in touch with the SAP SuccessFactors HR Innovation & Transformation team. Send a message to saphit@sap.com or reach out to me via email kristine.reyes@sap.com .

entry.title: From Prototype to Practice - or What Happens After an AI Hackathon
entry.content:
On July 14, 2026, we held the first combined alumni community session for participants of the SAP "Business meets AI" hackathons. Around 22 people joined from companies across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland. Three teams from Basler Verkehrsbetriebe (BVB), Viessmann Clean & Cold Solutions/Vexxus, and Frutura shared their experiences and where their use cases stand today. The session was set-up as a community based peer exchange. 

Where Do Hackathon Teams Stand Today?

We started with a quick Mentimeter pulse check. Most participants placed themselves somewhere between "Pilots" and "Scaling" in their AI transformation journey. When asked what happened to their hackathon prototypes, the majority answered "working on it"  somewhere between the initial POC and a productive system.

What helped most: support from the SAP coaches, a full week of focused time, getting a feeling for what is actually possible, and having a solid system landscape in place. 

The word cloud captured how people experienced the hackathon: "inspiration" came up most frequently, followed by "productive," "valuable," "enlightening," and "fun." (Actually this is a fantastic feedback :-)).

Basler Verkehrsbetriebe: AI-Supported Maintenance Orders for Life Cycle Costing

Ina Stern from Basler Verkehrs-Betriebe (BVB) works in the SAP team responsible for maintenance processes. BVB operates public transport in Basel, Switzerland, and e.g. performs life cycle costing for their bus fleet with manufacturers. Every maintenance activity needs to be billed and manufacturers have very specific requirements for how maintenance orders must be structured.

The  challenge: Creating these orders involves quiet some manual effort. Requirements are complex, mistakes can happen and rework is common. The team searched for a standard SAP solution in 2025, didn't find one, but learned about the hackathon format from an SAP contact.

The solution: The team built a conversational AI using Joule that performs similarity searches on custom tables extracted from their backend. Operators provide keywords — a component name, an error type, a specific condition and the system recommends how to structure the next maintenance order based on historical data. Because these are custom tables (not SAP standard data), the similarity search approach was essential.

The hackathon experience: BVB had no developers in-house and needed to find external support close to the deadline. They succeeded and won the technology prize at the Zurich hackathon in March 2026. Ina described the focused week as a real highlight: the team was impressed by how much they could achieve with that level of focus.

Current status of the use case: Not yet live. The two blockers are budget (the hackathon wasn't planned when last year's budget was set) and resource conflicts as the same team is currently improving another part of the process. The goal remains go-live by mid-to-end 2026.

Organizational impact: The internal visibility of the hackathon win triggered broader interest. Colleagues who were already thinking about AI felt encouraged to try. Several teams now want to participate in a future hackathon with their own use cases. As Ina put it: "We gave them some kind of push to actually go and try with AI." (I recorded a podcast with Ina in german language - check it out on Apple I Spotify)

Viessmann/Vexxus: Goods Receipt Optimization with DocumentAI and Process Automation

Oliver Gresch from Vexuxs (Viessmanns Clean & Cold Solutions internal IT partner, 100% Viessmann-owned) presented their goods receipt process optimization. The team won the WOW prize at the Munich hackathon.
