The starting point: Viessmann runs SAP S/4HANA 2022, where most standard AI agents are not yet available. They came to the hackathon somewhat skeptical about what they could achieve given their infrastructure constraints.

The solution: The core process runs in SAP Build Process Automation. AI enters at two specific points: first, Document AI reads the delivery note — the paper document arriving with each parcel and kicks off the process. Second, a LLM via the AI Launchpad handles a prompt-based task using interfaces to both SAP and Google systems. The result is a mobile-friendly goods receipt process that one person can handle independently, end to end. 

Organizational caution: The team came back enthusiastic, the organization was rather reserved. Trust in SAP as an AI platform was low at the time, partly triggered by SAP's stock price decline. And Google Workspace is already deeply embedded in the company.

The generic approach strategy: Oliver's response was to abstract the pattern: LLMs read and generate text. The workflow is deterministic. The same logic can later transition to an agent architecture. By framing it generically — "look at what LLMs do best, not at one specific use case" and by identifying similar processes across the organization, the team is gradually building internal support. (I would add: we see many use cases involving DocumentAI as one pattern to use AI for process automation).

Current status: Not yet live, but the team is now identifying and preparing multiple similar use cases for step-by-step rollout. They're also waiting for Joule Studio 2.0 to become available for the transition from BPA-based workflows to proper agent architectures.

You can listen here to the podcast with Oliver and his colleague Wolfgang Schade on Apple I Spotify.

Frutura: SAP Health Check Agent: From the Vienna Hackathon to Production in Three Months

The Frutura team presented a very advanced use case and probably the fastest path from prototype to production we've seen so far. Frutura is Austria's main supplier of fruits and vegetables for a supermarket organization, with around 1,000 employees and logistics operations running nearly 24/7.

The problem: Their SAP system produces around 400,000 SMQ2 queue entries per day. About 60-80 of those require manual attention. The issue isn't workload — it's detection time. If an error in the queue remains unresolved, ERP and Extended Warehouse Management can become inconsistent. That can lead to incorrect planning, blocked goods receipts, or failed deliveries. Because logistics doesn't stop on weekends, problems can emerge at any time.

The solution: The SAP Health Check Agent continuously monitors relevant SAP queues, automatically analyzes errors, identifies critical issues before they affect logistics, and summarizes system health in a clear overview. It reduces the need for manual checks and lets operators focus on resolving the important issues.

Technical architecture: Built as a Node.js application with the SAP Cloud SDK. The agent framework runs inside the app and connects to SAP via OData for reading SMQ2 entries. Agent-to-agent communication connects it to additional SAP tools. A technical user with restricted authorization (only custom OData functions) handles system access through Cloud Connector and SAP Integration Suite.

The path to production:

  • Before the Vienna hackathon (April 2026): concept and use case design completed
  • During the hackathon week: first working prototype built
  • After the hackathon: prototype refined, adjustments for production readiness
  • Current status: beta phase is running with manually triggered tests on the productive system
  • Next step: fully automated go-live the following week (20th of July), running as a job between 5am and 8pm

The time restriction is intended as that's when most logistics activity happens, and it's when developers can safely monitor the agent's behavior and correct mistakes during the initial phase.

Future roadmap: The team plans to connect the agent to their Confluence documentation and to SAP notes for a richer knowledge base. They're also integrating it into their internal company AI platform "Dolores" giving SAP developers a cockpit to monitor the agent and trigger it manually. Eventually, the agent should expand beyond SMQ2 to other parts of the SAP ecosystem.

What Slows Teams Down After the Hackathon?

Across all three cases, common patterns emerge:

  • Budget timing: Hackathon participation wasn't planned in the previous year's budget cycle, creating funding gaps for productionization.
  • Resource conflicts: The same people who build AI prototypes are often tied up in other improvement projects.
  • Organizational skepticism: Returning with enthusiasm doesn't automatically translate into organizational commitment - especially when platform strategy decisions are challenged.
  • Technical readiness: Teams on older SAP releases (e.g., S/4HANA 2022) face limitations with embedded AI features and are waiting for new tooling like Joule Studio 2.0.

What's Next: Agent Hackathons, Public Sector, and Custom AI Services

SAP Agent Hackathon — Joule Studio 2.0 (November/December 2026)

Philipp Sütterlin introduced the next evolution of the hackathon format: building agent use cases directly on customer environments with the new Joule Studio 2.0. The format covers the full journey — from use case discovery and agent design through system setup to four days of hacking, with go-live support afterwards. Three locations, each with around 10 customer teams:

  • Frankfurt: November 10–13, 2026
  • Vienna: November 17–20, 2026
  • Munich: December 1–4, 2026

Michael Dohse from tesa SE, who experienced Joule Studio 2.0 at a preview hackathon in Potsdam in May, shared his assessment: the development speed and deep SAP integration represent a significant step forward compared to the first generation of Joule Studio. If you're interested, apply early as there are technical prerequisites that SAP experts need to verify before confirming participation.

Registration: SAP Agent Hackathon Sign-up 

German Public Sector & Utilities Hackathon (November 2026)

For customers from public sector and utilities industries: a dedicated hackathon running November 9–13 in Potsdam, conducted in German language. This follows the successful Berlin edition from March 2026. Registration & Info can be found here. 

Customer-specific AI Applications (CAIA) 

For teams that need hands-on technical support beyond the hackathon, SAP's Custom AI Innovation Services offer a phased approach: scoping workshops with functional, industry, and AI experts; proof of concepts based on customer data; and full solution delivery operated by SAP to product standards. Some of the CAIA consultants already served as coaches during the hackathons, so for many alumni, these might are familiar faces. If your prototype needs development support from SAP to reach production, this is the path to explore. (Just contact your SAP Services contact or reach out to us). 

Quotes in the session 

"We (with the hackathon experience) gave them some kind of push to actually go and try with AI." Ina Stern, BVB Basel, on the organizational ripple effect

"The trust to SAP was not extremely high at that time. We had to convince people that SAP, with its tools, is able to bring value into process improvement." Oliver Gresch, Vexxus/Viessmann

"We plan to start next week with the fully automated release of our solution." — Frutura team, three months after the hackathon

"After seeing Joule Studio 2.0, I'm totally convinced that this will be the next big thing." Michael Dohse, tesa SE

"A community is not an audience. Lets meet, discuss and share aexperiences." Guiding principle of the alumni session 

Practical Tips for Moving from Prototype to Production

  • Describe the use case generically: If others in your organization can see the pattern applies to more than one process, buy-in increases. Oliver's approach at Viessmann is a good example for this.
  • Secure budget proactively: Plan hackathon follow-up costs (innovation/ automation budget) in the next budget cycle if possible. Ina's experience at BVB shows this. And we know that this budget is important, but not always easy to get, esp. during the year if not budgeted.  
  • Start with a controlled scope: Frutura's approach of limiting automated agent operation to specific hours depending on possible human oversightht reduces risk and builds trust with stakeholders. Its also really pragmatic. 
  • Use the hackathon for internal communication: The visibility and awards create momentum. Share results broadly, as it pushes people who were already considering AI to actually start. And it shows the possibilities of AI which are so broad. 
  • Don't wait for perfect tooling: Use what's available today (Build Process Automation, Doc AI, AI Launchpad) and design your architecture so you can migrate to agent frameworks later.
  • Get support where needed: Whether it's the Agent Hackathon for hands-on Joule Studio 2.0 experience or CAIA for professional development support, use the formats SAP offers to accelerate your path.

How shall we go on?

When asked "​How often do you want to meet?" 80% said " Quarterly - so will align to this and meet back  in October.

We also asked  "What do you want to do in the next session?"  most voted for an Expert-Demo for Joule Studio 2.0, follwed by a 
Promptathon with new Tools as well as experience sharing. Sounds like a great plan for the next three sessions. Come to the Alumni LinkedIn group  to also exchange digitally - thanks to the Swiss team who opened it). 

My Take

This was the first Alumni Session I moderated and I was especially impressed by all the expertise and the open sharing of experiences as well open discussions. We also see that challenges often are organizational and human, e.g. budget cycles, resource allocation, or trust. The hackathon creates energy and motivation about what's possible and is a great format and boost for an AI Transformation. 

I am looking forward to the next quarterly session, probably with an interactive Joule Studio 2.0 demo and more experience reports from teams that made it to production. 

FAQ

Is Joule Studio 2.0 available now? Not yet for general availability. SAP is onboarding customers through the Agent Hackathon format (November/December 2026). Apply via the sign-up page to get the process started, SAP will check technical prerequisites before confirming participation.

Can teams switch from Build Process Automation to agents later? Yes. Oliver from Viessmann described their approach: since the workflow logic remains the same, you can start with deterministic BPA processes today and migrate to an agent architecture when the tooling matures. The same APIs and logic apply as agents just remove the deterministic constraint. (my take: you also do not always need agents to automate processes).

How often will this community meet? Quarterly. The group voted for this cadence. Next session likely in October 2026, with a Joule Studio 2.0 expert demo and further experience sharing on the agenda.

Mentioned Resources & Links

  • SAP Agent Hackathon — Frankfurt, Vienna, Munich (Nov/Dec 2026) | Registration & Info
  • German Public Sector & Utilities Hackathon — November 9-13, 2026 in Potsdam |  Registration & Info
  • SAP Zurich Hackathon — September 14-18, 2026 | ​Registration & Info
  • Customer-specific AI Applications (CAIA) — Custom AI development by SAP | Contact your SAP services representative
  • ​SAPNews: KI-Hackathons als Innovationsmotor: von der Idee zum MVP incl. many use case descriptions 
  • Hackathons als Innovationsmotor und Antreiber von AI Transformationen mit Alexander Finger – Apple I Spotify
  • Vom Hackathon zur Praxis: KI bei den Basler Verkehrs-Betrieben mit Ina Stern – Apple I Spotify
  • Viessmann Clean & Cold Solutions Erfahrungen beim SAP Business meets AI Hackathon – Apple I Spotify
  • Erfahrungen im SAP Hackathon zu KI im Vertrieb bei Sto – Apple I Spotify
  • LEONI Video - Use Case HR Flow AI, going live summer 2026 
  • Alumni LinkedIn Group - Cross-cohort networking, opened by the Swiss team

entry.title: What's new in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release
entry.content:
When you trace the evolution of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, a pattern emerges: every release compounds our momentum; continuous innovation on a solid core.

We’ve broadened business scope, deepened our ecosystem, simplified upgrades, and scaled across industries and regions. With the release of 2608, we’re taking the next confident step.

Introducing the Autonomous Enterprise at SAP Sapphire, Business AI moves from concept to companion (you are welcome to follow the link Autonomous Enterprise | SAP) — not a driver, but an intelligent co‑worker embedded in the ERP experience. It helps the business users work faster and smarter while they stay firmly in control. The best decisions still come from people; AI simply helps them get there sooner.

Marking our 35th major release in SaaS ERP, we’re pairing Business AI with continued substantive progress across the core of the business: Finance and Manufacturing, Engineering and Workplace Safety, and Professional Services. All that keeps our customers’ business moving.

 

As always, I wish I had more time to show you everything we've delivered in this release. So, take this video as an appetizer for our upcoming deep-dive blogs, where my colleagues and I will walk you through many more innovations across various lines of business.

 

 

SAP-managed scenarios

Before we start with Business AI, let me share some of the latest capabilities around SAP-managed scenarios. I believe this is something that delivers significant value to every customer who adopts more than just core finance processes, which is the case for most of our customers. We started introducing SAP-managed scenarios with 2602 and continue to expand the portfolio with additional scenarios, where SAP takes care of the setup and interoperability across the suite of solutions. Our Suite Product Management team has done a fantastic job in this area, delivering the first scenarios with big success.

Since the 2602 release, Early Adopter customers and partners have successfully adopted the first SAP-managed business scenarios:

  • SAP-managed Workforce Management enables seamless Hire to Retire processes based on consistent workforce and financial data across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP SuccessFactors.
  • SAP-managed Operational Procurement provides a connected Procure to Pay experience through SAP-managed integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Ariba Buying, and SAP Ariba Invoicing.

More SAP-managed innovation with 2608:

Enhancement of SAP-managed Hire to Retire

  • Expansion of SAP-managed Hire to Retire with SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding and SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking, extending end-to-end process coverage across the employee lifecycle.
  • Consolidated Time Recording for Professional Services, improving the user experience for time and activity recording.
  • Contingent Workers Management across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP SuccessFactors throughout the worker lifecycle.

Early Adopter Care for SAP-managed Lead to Cash

  • Early Adopter Care for SAP-managed integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, enabling a connected Sales Force Automation process with consistent data, improved sales visibility, and a seamless experience across front-office and back-office operations.

Strengthening SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as the core

  • SAP-managed integration with SAP Subscription Billing and SAP Market Rates Management. Embedded into the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition user experience, these capabilities enable seamless cross-solution processes from a single launchpad.
  • SAP-managed integration with SAP Taulia extends working capital management processes, helping customers reduce integration complexity and connect finance and treasury operations more seamlessly across solutions.


But now, let's move on to Business AI. Across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we're introducing specialized AI assistants and agents that automate routine tasks, provide intelligent recommendations, and support users in their everyday work. Here are a few highlights that stood out to me.

 

Business AI innovations

Before we dig deeper into the topic of individual Business AI innovations, let me make a clarifying statement.

Business AI and Business users are designed to be a duet, not a duel.

AI agents are becoming an increasingly important part of our product evolution. We are seeing an increasing number of specialized AI agents supporting business tasks, while users remain firmly in control of business decisions and approvals. Agents help automate repetitive activities, provide intelligent recommendations, and work together behind the scenes to make everyday business processes more efficient. This lifts the execution level while the business user is still in control. This very approach, combining intelligent automation with human expertise, you will see reflected throughout the 2608 release.

Here are a few agents that I am excited about in the 2608 release:

 

SAP User Experience

AI-assisted easy fill of SAP Fiori elements-based applications

We want everyone to feel at home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, so we’re making the user experience easier to learn and quicker to master. That’s why we created Fast with SAP Fiori: short, one‑minute videos that show you exactly how to get things done, from working with tables to setting default values. You can find the first 10 tips right in the Help menu of the SAP Fiori launchpad, with more on the way. They’re easy to watch, easy to share from the SAP Digital Library, and available in different languages. Don’t see the tip you need? Just send us an improvement request in the SAP CIP session “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, User Experience and Cross‑Topics.”

We’re also expanding our AI-assisted UX. I am happy to announce the general availability of the initial version of AI-assisted easy fill. Business users often spend unnecessary time transferring information from emails, meeting notes, or phone calls into ERP transactions—an effort that is both repetitive and prone to manual errors. Users can now simply describe the business update in natural language. SAP automatically populates the relevant fields, making data entry faster, more intuitive, and less prone to errors. It's a great example of how Business AI is simplifying everyday work and making SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition easier to use.

This feature is one of 17 base AI-assisted features available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608. If not yet done, make sure to sign the "Acceptance of SAP AI terms for Base AI-assisted features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" to use the generally available base features at no additional cost. 

If you want to be part of the beta testing of the latest AI innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, kindly sign-up here. 

 

Finance & Risk

Receivables & Payables Clearing Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Let’s continue with Finance. Anyone who worked in this function knows how much time disappears into matching and clearing open receivables and payables. With our new Receivables and Payables Clearing Agent, that work changes fundamentally. It analyses open items, proposes balanced clearing entries, explains every recommendation, and, once approved, supports automated posting. The outcome: a faster, more accurate clearing process that lifts finance productivity and helps shorten the financial close.

 

International Trade Classification Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Classifying products for international trade is a complex and highly regulated process that requires significant expertise and careful interpretation of customs regulations. The International Trade Classification Agent leverages Business AI to analyze product information, interpret trade regulations, and recommend appropriate customs classification codes together with the legal reasoning behind each recommendation. This helps trade specialists classify products more efficiently, create auditable decisions, reduce compliance risks, and strengthen supply chain resilience while significantly reducing the effort required for classification.

 

Statutory Report Analysis Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Preparing statutory reports often requires finance teams to manually compare reporting periods, investigate unexpected variances, and trace the root causes of unusual values across tax codes, business partners, and financial documents. The new Statutory Report Analysis Agent helps finance organizations review statutory reports faster, identify anomalies earlier, and improve compliance before submission. The AI agent automates this analysis by detecting outliers, identifying trends and irregularities, generating concise summaries of key findings, and recommending next steps for further investigation or validation. By reducing manual review effort and providing explainable, data-driven insights, the Statutory Report Analysis Agent helps organizations improve reporting accuracy, accelerate the declaration process, identify potential compliance risks earlier, and make more informed decisions before submitting statutory reports.

 

 

E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent (planned for 2608.2)

As e-invoicing mandates continue to expand worldwide, finance teams increasingly need to process and troubleshoot submission errors returned by tax authorities and e-invoicing networks under tight regulatory deadlines. The E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent helps organizations quickly identify, understand, and resolve errors during electronic invoice submission. The AI agent automatically classifies technical and business-related errors, distinguishes between infrastructure, connectivity, data quality, and financial posting issues, and provides guided root cause analysis together with contextual resolution recommendations. By standardizing error handling, reducing manual investigation, and delivering explainable recommendations with humans in the loop, the agent helps organizations accelerate issue resolution, improve compliance with government e-invoicing requirements, and reduce the risk of submission delays and regulatory penalties.

 

Manufacturing

Production Planning & Operations Agent

Manufacturing is another space where AI moves the needle. Our new Production Planning and Operations Agent equips supervisors to manage orders with greater confidence: it automatically checks component availability, flags scheduling conflicts, and even supports order release via natural language. By cutting routine manual work and surfacing the right insights at the right time, it lifts productivity, reduces downtime, and keeps operations running smoothly.

 

Further innovations across various LoBs

SAP Integrated Product Development

Handover from Formulation Recipe to S/4HANA Master Recipe

Bringing new products from development into manufacturing often requires manual handovers and multiple data transfers, increasing the risk of inconsistencies and delays. Handover to Master Recipe in SAP Integrated Product Development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition streamlines this transition by enabling production engineers to derive production-ready master recipes directly from product development data. The innovation automates the creation of master recipes, bills of materials (BOMs), and production versions while preserving the relationship between engineering and manufacturing data. By reducing manual effort and ensuring greater consistency across product development and production planning, organizations can accelerate manufacturing readiness, improve process reliability, and support a more seamless transition from product design to execution. For more information, consult our SAP Help Portal.

 

Sales

Supply block in a sales order to control subsequent activities

A single sales order can trigger a cascade of downstream activities, from available-to-promise (ATP) checks and material requirements planning (MRP) to logistics execution. Supply block in a sales order to control subsequent activities helps organizations ensure these processes only begin when order data is complete, validated, and ready for execution. This innovation introduces an automated, item-level supply block that can be configured based on business rules, approval workflows, credit checks, or custom validation logic. By temporarily pausing subsequent activities until all required validations have been completed, organizations can reduce operational risk, minimize manual intervention, improve visibility into order status, and help ensure more accurate, reliable, and efficient order fulfillment.

 

Environment, Health, and Safety

Maintain and view hazardous substance inventory

Another innovation focuses on workplace safety and regulatory compliance. The new Maintain and View Hazardous Substance Inventory applications provide a centralized view of hazardous substances, associated products, and safety information across the enterprise. Users can create and maintain comprehensive hazardous substance inventories, organize data by business area or location, manage inventory revisions, and quickly access key details such as hazardous ingredients and Globally Harmonized System (GHS) classifications. This helps organizations strengthen compliance, improve workplace safety, and manage safety-critical information more efficiently.

 

Professional Services

New and improved time-recording app for professional services projects with a smooth mobile experience

Let’s close with professional services. The redesigned Time Recording app brings a modern, consistent experience across desktop and mobile, with flexible entry, bulk edits, and personalized views that make logging project time fast and effortless. The payoff is clear: more accurate timesheets, less administrative work, and stronger billing and resource planning.

   

                                         

 

Service

Customer and product hierarchy pricing

Every customer expects repair pricing to be transparent, accurate, and aligned with the agreements they have negotiated. Customer Hierarchy and Product Hierarchy Related Pricing in In-House Repair Transactions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 enables organizations to apply pricing and discounts consistently across in-house repair processes based on customer and product hierarchies. By automatically determining the appropriate pricing conditions for different customer groups and product structures, the innovation eliminates manual pricing adjustments and helps ensure that negotiated agreements are applied correctly throughout the repair process. The result is more accurate invoicing, greater pricing consistency, an improved customer experience, and a smoother connection between service execution and billing.

 

Cross Topics – Data and Analytics

Central governance of product data and business partners including customer and supplier segments

High-quality master data is the foundation of every successful ERP system. The central governance of product data and business partners helps organizations establish trusted product, customer, supplier, and business partner data through workflow-driven governance, configurable validation rules, automated field value derivation, and flexible approval processes powered by SAP Build Process Automation. By improving data quality from the point of entry and reducing manual maintenance, organizations can streamline governance, strengthen compliance, and enable more reliable business processes and better decision-making across the enterprise.

 

 

Smart business runtime environment

The Smart business runtime environment introduces AI-generated summaries that help business users understand KPIs and reports faster. Business users often spend significant time analyzing dashboards, drilling down into reports, and interpreting large volumes of business data before they can identify trends or make informed decisions. With the Smart Business Runtime Environment, users can automatically generate concise, contextual summaries for SAP Smart Business KPIs and reports directly within SAP Fiori, highlighting key insights, anomalies, and important business trends. By transforming complex analytics into clear, shareable narratives, the innovation accelerates insight consumption, simplifies data interpretation, and enables faster, more informed decision-making across the organization.

 

Closing thoughts

Every release is an opportunity to make SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition smarter, simpler, and more valuable for our customers.

On one hand, we’re expanding the possibilities of Business AI with intelligent assistants and specialized agents. On the other, we’re doubling down on the core capabilities that help organizations run better every day.

Those above are my key innovations in the 2608 Release Highlights. I’m keen to hear what matters most to you.

I hope this introduction gave you some appetizers of what’s new — and spotlighted a few innovations you’ll want to explore further. For deeper insights, check out the “What's New in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” SAP Help page and join the conversation on SAP Community to share which innovations you’re most excited about.

In the coming weeks, we'll take a closer look at additional business- and industry-specific innovations in a series of follow-up posts here on SAP Community.  To stay up to date with future content, be sure to follow the PSCC_Enablement tag and keep an eye on upcoming posts.

Feel free to follow me at BeSchulze in the community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates. 

Until next time,

Bert

 

 

🎦Register for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series! 

Between July 13-17, we are hosting a series of 24 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. On our landing page you can register for the entire series or pick the sessions most relevant to you. 

🔎Find all our assets  

The release 2608 showcases more than 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: Work Smarter with AI-Assisted Tasks in the SAP Activate Project Management Workstream
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series highlighting AI capabilities in SAP Activate. For additional information, visit the main blog here. 

Project Managers are expected to balance strategic planning with day-to-day execution while keeping projects on schedule, within budget, and aligned with business objectives. To help streamline these activities, the SAP Activate road map viewer now includes AI-assisted procedures for tasks within the Project Management workstream.

These AI-Assisted procedures are designed to help project managers quickly create high-quality project deliverables by combining SAP Activate best practices with generative AI. Rather than starting from a blank page, project teams can leverage guided AI prompts to accelerate planning, improve consistency, and reduce manual effort.

What Are AI-Assisted Procedures?

AI-Assisted procedures provide step-by-step guidance for using generative AI to complete common project management activities. Each procedure includes a structured prompt that incorporates SAP Activate methodology and best practices, helping generate a tailored starting point that can be reviewed and refined for your specific project.

The generated content is intended to support project teams by providing a solid first draft while allowing project managers to apply their expertise and project-specific knowledge before finalizing the deliverable.

The following Project Management tasks now include AI-Assisted procedures with the SAP Activate road map viewer:

Develop a Project Charter

A well-defined project charter establishes the project’s purpose, objectives, scope, governance, and success criteria. The AI-Assisted procedure helps generate a comprehensive project charter by incorporating information such as project goals, stakeholders, timelines, assumptions, constraints, risks, and governance structure.

This provides project managers with a structured draft that can be customized to meet the organization's specific requirements.

Estimate Backlog Items

Accurate backlog estimation is essential for planning releases and managing project delivery. The AI-Assisted procedure assists teams in developing consistent effort estimates by analyzing backlog item descriptions and applying estimation guidance based on SAP Activate best practices.

The resulting estimates can be reviewed and refined by the project team during backlog and sprint planning activities.

Develop a Hypercare Support Plan

Preparing for a post-go-live support is critical to ensuring a successful transition into operations. The AI-Assisted procedure helps generate a structured Hypercare Support plan that outlines support objectives, roles and responsibilities, issue management processes, communication plans, monitoring activities, escalation paths, and exit criteria.

This provides implementation teams with a comprehensive starting point for planning the Hypercare period.

Handover Operations Responsibility

A smooth transition from the implementation project to the operational support organization is key to long-term success. The AI-Assisted procedure assists project teams in developing an operational handover plan that defines transition activities, knowledge transfer, documentation requirements, support responsibilities, and operational readiness.

The generated plan helps ensure that operational teams receive the information they need to effectively support the solution after go-live.

Communicate Project Status and Progress

Keeping stakeholders informed with timely, accurate, and consistent project updates is essential for effective project governance. The AI-Assisted procedure helps project managers create a comprehensive project status report by generating a structured summary of overall project health, milestone progress, schedule and budget status, key accomplishments, risks and issues, upcoming activities, and decisions requiring stakeholder attention.

This provides project managers with a professional, SAP Activate-aligned starting point that can be reviewed, tailored with project specific information, and shared with stakeholders to support informed decision making and transparent project communication.

Why Use AI-Assisted Procedures?

By incorporating AI-Assisted procedures into the SAP Activate methodology project teams can:

  • Accelerate the creation of common project management deliverables.
  • Start with structured, SAP Activate-aligned content instead of a blank document.
  • Improve consistency across projects by leveraging standardized guidance.
  • Reduce the time spent drafting project documentation.
  • Allow project managers to focus more on project leadership, stakeholder engagement, and decision-making.

These procedures are designed to enhance, not replace, the expertise of experienced project managers. Human review remains an essential part of the process to validate accuracy, tailor the content to the project, and ensure alignment with organizational standards.

The AI-Assisted Procedure tag helps you quickly identify tasks that include guidance for using generative AI. These tasks contain an AI-Assisted procedure and a link to the SAP Activate Prompt Library, making it easy to generate first drafts of project deliverables while following SAP Activate best practices.

entry.title: AI-Assisted Extensibility Planning & Design in SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series that explains how to leverage SAP Business AI in your implementation projects. For additional context, please refer to the main blog post, SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions of AI for Your Implementation

Clean Core and Extensibility in SAP Activate

SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP Private includes a dedicated Clean Core Success Plan that spans all project phases. The methodology establishes clean core governance early. A Solution Standardization Board is set up during Prepare, extensibility scenarios are evaluated and classified before detailed design begins, and Clean Core Quality Gates run through the project to track progress against clean core objectives.

For extensibility, SAP's clean core approach distinguishes between two architectural patterns. On-stack extensibility covers extensions that run directly on SAP S/4HANA and require tight coupling with core data, transactions, or applications. Side-by-side extensibility on SAP BTP covers loosely coupled extensions that interact with the core through released remote APIs and events. When planning extensions, SAP recommends starting with what SAP BTP can offer, as this supports upgrade readiness and innovation potential. Where business or technical requirements clearly call for proximity to the core, on-stack extensions remain the right choice.

On-stack extensions should be built using the ABAP Cloud development model, which enforces clean core compliance through compiler checks and restricts usage to publicly released SAP APIs and extension points. Classic extensibility (custom ABAP development using SAP internal objects or modifications) is still available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise, but carries higher upgrade risk and sits at the lower end of the clean core level scale. The goal is to reach Level A extensions built with SAP Build using only publicly released, stable interfaces, and to eliminate Level D extensions that use non-recommended objects, modifications, or direct write access to SAP tables.

The extensibility workstream in the Explore phase is where most of the documentation work happens: consolidating requirements from fit-to-standard workshops, producing functional specifications for each RICEFW object, and then moving into detailed technical design for those that need custom development. These are necessary steps, but they are time-consuming.

AI-Assisted Procedures in SAP Activate

SAP Activate now includes AI-Assisted Procedures for selected tasks across the SAP Cloud ERP Private - New Implementation roadmap. Each one is embedded directly in the task description in the SAP Roadmap Viewer and connects to a specific prompt in the SAP Activate Prompt Library, which is hosted within the Roadmap Viewer.

Tasks with an AI-Assisted Procedure are tagged in the SAP Roadmap Viewer with two tags: SAP Business AI and AI-Assisted Procedure. These tags can be used to filter the roadmap and quickly identify all tasks that carry AI-assisted guidance, without navigating through the full task list.

For the extensibility workstream, three of these AI-Assisted Procedures support the documentation and design effort leading up to development.

 

SAP Joule for Consultants

SAP Joule for Consultants is the SAP Business AI capability used to execute the prompts discussed in this blog. It is the SAP Consulting capability within Joule, available to consultants working on SAP engagements, that provides access to expert templates and project-specific prompt execution. Consultants provide their project inputs, attach relevant documents where required, and SAP Joule for Consultants generates a structured first-draft deliverable ready for review and refinement.

The AI-Assisted Procedures in this blog all target SAP Joule for Consultants. SAP Activate is expanding the scope of SAP Business AI across the methodology, and future procedures will leverage additional Joule and SAP Business AI capabilities beyond the consulting capability described here. For more information, see the SAP Joule for Consultants Service Guide.

Using the SAP Activate Prompt Library

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is accessible directly from the SAP Roadmap Viewer. Each prompt entry is structured to give consultants everything they need to run the prompt and understand the expected output before they start. Each entry includes the following fields:

Field Description

Prompt NameThe name of the prompt, which corresponds to the deliverable it produces.
RoadmapThe SAP Activate roadmap or roadmaps the prompt applies to.
TaskThe specific task in the roadmap where the prompt is used.
Target JouleThe Joule capability used to execute the prompt.
Expert TemplateAn optional expert template that structures the Joule session for the specific use case.
Attachments / RequirementsThe documents required or recommended as input when running the prompt.
Sample OutputA worked example showing the format and depth of the deliverable the prompt produces.

The prompt library is the starting point before running any AI-Assisted Procedure. Reviewing the sample output for the relevant prompt gives consultants a clear picture of what SAP Joule for Consultants will produce and what inputs are needed.

 

Extensibility Design and Planning Prompts within SAP Activate

Document Extensibility Requirements

The "Document Extensibility Requirements" task in the Explore phase is where all extensibility needs identified during fit-to-standard workshops get formally documented, classified, and signed off by the Solution Standardization Board before design begins. Producing a complete Functional Specification for each RICEFW object (covering the gap justification, process flows, authorization requirements, data model considerations, and test conditions) is the core output of this task and typically requires significant consultant time per object.

SAP Activate Task: Document Extensibility Requirements
SAP Joule for Consultants Prompt: Functional Specification Document

Takes the RICEFW object list from the workshops as its primary input, with the SAP Activate Lean Specifications Template as an optional attachment. Produces a complete, structured FSD for each object covering gap analysis, functional description, authorization matrix, process flows, data model, dependencies, and test scenarios in a format directly usable in MS Word.

Detail Design of Extension with Classic Extensibility Techniques

Once requirements are documented and clean-core alternatives are confirmed as insufficient, the detailed technical design for each classic extension needs to be produced. This requires evaluating the extension against SAP's Clean Core Extensibility framework and producing a specification precise enough for development: entry points, APIs or extension technologies, enhancement logic, data model changes, and authorizations.

SAP Activate Task: Detail Design of Extension with Classic Extensibility Techniques
SAP Joule for Consultants Prompt: Design Analysis and Technical Specification

Takes the Functional Specification document from the previous task as its input. For each RICEFW object, it determines the clean core compliance level with justification, then generates the full technical design specification: entry point, released API or extension technology, enhancement logic and process flow, data model considerations, and authorization requirements. Where a cleaner alternative exists or should be evaluated in a future release, the output flags it.

These two prompts connect directly. The FSD produced by the first is the mandatory input for the second. The RICEFW object list from workshops flows through to a complete technical design specification with one human review step in between.

Development of Clean Core Compliant Extensions

Before development begins, all extension objects need consistent, project-specific technical names that comply with the agreed customer namespace, Clean Core tier, and SAP naming rules. Without this, naming decisions get made ad hoc by individual developers, leading to inconsistencies across workstreams that are difficult to correct once objects exist in the system.

SAP Activate Task: Development of Clean Core Compliant Extensions
SAP Joule for Consultants Prompt: Extensibility Naming Conventions

Takes the project RICEFW List, a sample Functional Specification Document header, and the project Extensibility Blueprint as inputs. Produces a complete, project-ready Extensibility Naming Convention document covering all three Clean Core extensibility tiers: Developer, Key User, and Side-by-Side, with naming patterns, length constraints, examples, and counter-examples for every object type. Includes a Complete Object Naming Register that validates every object from the RICEFW List against the agreed conventions and flags deviations.

All three prompts connect as a chain. The RICEFW object list from workshops flows through to a complete technical design specification and a validated naming convention document, with one human review step between each stage.

Step 1: Document Extensibility Requirements
Functional Specification Document prompt → FSD per RICEFW object

FSD output becomes mandatory input for Step 2

Step 2: Detail Design of Extension with Classic Extensibility Techniques
Design Analysis and Technical Specification prompt → Clean Core assessment + Technical Design Spec

Technical Design Spec and RICEFW List become inputs for Step 3

Step 3: Development of Clean Core Compliant Extensions
Extensibility Naming Conventions prompt → Project-ready Naming Convention document + Complete Object Naming Register

All three prompts are available in the SAP Activate Prompt Library.

What's Next

The SAP Activate team is actively identifying more ways to leverage SAP Business AI within the SAP Activate methodology to accelerate the implementation of SAP solutions. The extensibility prompts are part of a broader effort to embed AI assistance at the points in a project where documentation and decision-making effort is highest. With the addition of the Extensibility Naming Conventions prompt, the AI-assisted extensibility coverage now extends into the Realize phase, supporting naming governance before development begins.

All three prompts are accessible in the SAP Activate Prompt Library within the SAP Roadmap Viewer: Functional Specification Document, Design Analysis and Technical Specification, and Extensibility Naming Conventions.

Related Resources

  • SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer
  • SAP Joule for Consultants Service Guide
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Expert Portal
  • Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Transform Your Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation with AI-Assisted Procedures
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series highlighting AI capabilities in SAP Activate. For an overview of the complete series, visit the main blog post here: SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions ... - SAP Community

What if AI could help your project team prepare Fit-to-Standard workshops faster, more consistently, and with greater confidence?

Now it can.

Why Fit-to-Standard preparation matters

Preparing for Fit-to-Standard workshops is one of the most important activities in an SAP implementation.

Before the workshops begin, the project team must:

  • Understand and validate the implementation scope.
  • Map the business processes and solutions to be demonstrated.
  • Identify the appropriate stakeholders.
  • Define clear roles and responsibilities.
  • Prepare a detailed workshop agenda.
  • Determine the required inputs and expected outcomes.

This preparation is essential—but it can also require significant time and coordination.

What if an AI-assisted procedure could help the project team perform these activities?

We have exciting news.

AI assistance is now embedded in SAP Activate

SAP Activate now includes AI-Assisted Procedures for selected tasks within the Application: Design and Configuration workstream of the SAP Cloud ERP Private – New Implementation roadmap.

These procedures combine SAP Activate best practices with generative AI to help project teams create comprehensive Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation and planning materials more efficiently.

Each AI-Assisted Procedure is:

  • Embedded directly in the task description in SAP Roadmap Viewer.
  • Connected to a task-specific prompt.
  • Supported by the SAP Activate Prompt Library.
  • Designed to fit naturally into the implementation workflow.

This means project teams can access AI guidance precisely where they need it—without leaving SAP Roadmap Viewer.

Let’s explore “Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops”

Open the Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops task in SAP Roadmap Viewer.

Inside the task, you will find an AI-Assisted Procedure that guides you through the workshop preparation process. Open the procedure and follow steps 1 through 9.

The procedure helps you organize the necessary project information and use the corresponding prompt to generate a structured Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation plan.

Two new accelerators to support the process

We have also created two accelerators to help project teams use the procedure and its prompt more effectively:

  1. Fit-to-Standard Workshop Notes — a structured template for capturing the information required to prepare and run the prompt.
  2. SAP Activate Prompt Library — a collection of prompts supporting multiple activities across the SAP Activate roadmaps, including the Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops prompt.

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is available directly from the SAP Roadmap Viewer. Simply click the prompt referenced in the task to access it.

Each prompt entry provides consultants with the information they need to:

  • Understand the purpose of the prompt.
  • Identify the required inputs.
  • Execute the prompt correctly.
  • Review the expected output before getting started.

A structured prompt based on the COP Framework

The prompts follow the COP Prompt Framework, which is organized around:

  • Context — the project background and information the AI needs.
  • Objectives — the results the prompt should produce.
  • Preferences — the expected structure, level of detail, and output format.

This framework makes the prompts easier to understand, execute, and adapt to the specific needs of each project.

What does the prompt generate?

The Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation Plan prompt can generate:

  1. Scope Validation Checklist — a pre-workshop sign-off checklist for the System Integrator Lead and Customer Project Manager.
  2. Business Process Scoping Matrix — maps in-scope functional areas to SAP Best Practices scope item IDs, workshop dates, and business owners.
  3. Business Stakeholder Pre-Work — an actionable checklist of items due at least five business days before each workshop.
  4. System Readiness Checklist — covers sandbox provisioning, SAP Best Practices activation, master data, user access, and SAP Cloud ALM setup.
  5. Workshop Agenda Templates — includes a general full-day Fit-to-Standard agenda and tailored versions for functional areas such as Finance, Procure-to-Pay, Order-to-Cash, Manufacturing, Warehouse Management, and Asset Management.
  6. Fit-to-Standard Tracking Template — provides a detailed gap log, including one row for each delta requirement identified in the workshop notes.
  7. Roles and RACI Matrix — defines accountability across key customer, system integrator, architecture, consulting, and project management roles.
  8. Gap Capture Guidance for SAP Cloud ALM — provides a step-by-step process for recording gaps, completing mandatory attributes, assigning resolution categories, and maintaining traceability.
  9. Risks and Mitigation Strategies — creates a risk register covering workshop execution, scope, stakeholders, and technical readiness.
  10. Classification Decision Tree — supports consistent classification of requirements as Fit, Partial Fit, Gap, Out of Scope, or Deferred.

A representative example is also available in the Sample Output section of the prompt library.

What are the benefits?

AI-Assisted Procedures can help project teams:

  • Accelerate Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation.
  • Generate agendas based on the project scope.
  • Improve visibility into required resources, stakeholders, and inputs.
  • Reduce the time needed to validate workshop topics and schedules.
  • Establish a structured timetable for the topics to be discussed.
  • Apply SAP Activate guidance more consistently.
  • Start from a comprehensive, structured plan that the project team can review and refine.

AI supports the preparation—but the project team remains responsible for reviewing, validating, and adapting the output to the project context.

This is just the beginning

AI-Assisted Procedures bring SAP Activate guidance and generative AI together directly within the implementation workflow.

And this is only the beginning—more AI-assisted content is on the way.

Are you ready to use the Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation prompt in your next project?

Try it and share your experience in the comments. We would love to learn how you used the prompt, what value it brought to your project, and which SAP Activate task you would like to see supported next.
